LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND JOSEPH MANNERY'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO RELY UPON PROVISIONS OTHER THAN TRAFFIC CODE SECTION 170.2-A, SHE MUST ALLEGE THEM IN THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF ALSO MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT DEMONSTRATES THAT THE CONDUCT COMPLAINED OF IS NOT SUBJECT TO THE IMMUNITY UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 818.8. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
